info.title: Minecraft Log Resolver API
info.description: Local API for generated Minecraft log reports, split report store metadata, refresh jobs, and rule-editor support.

get.summary: Report readiness and small overview, or report_not_ready/report_invalid_json/report_invalid_schema
First-run, setup state with nextActions, recovery actions, launcher contract, project, report/store readiness including missing store files, refresh, and refresh-needed status
Effective local app config and constrained local overlay metadata

put.summary: Save whitelisted local-only config fields to a safe local overlay

post.summary: Validate user-selected local Minecraft log roots before saving
Open a local Windows folder picker for first-run log root setup

200.description: Selected directory, cancelled picker, and optional root validation result.

get.summary: Generated report summary JSON, or summary_invalid_json/summary_invalid_schema
Full generated report JSON, or report_invalid_json/report_invalid_schema
Career/profile highlights and identity leaderboards for dashboard and share-card UI
Static machine-readable metric definitions for frontend display semantics
Continuous-mode activity segments such as The Pit
Paginated round rows with stable server display fields, result hints, and unknown audit filters
Game mode totals
Result coverage, signal debug data, unknown hints, and privacy-safe unknown audit queues
Mined candidate chat lines for improving result rules
Same-origin Minecraft skin texture proxy for the 3D viewer
Current refresh job status with phase, status, progress, total and per-phase duration, failure classification, cancellation, and log tail

post.summary: Start a background report refresh followed by split-store export
Request cancellation of the current refresh job

get.summary: Recently completed refresh jobs with summary counts, per-phase durations, failure classification, and non-fatal warnings for corrupt derived history

Lightweight local performance baseline, refresh diagnostics, output timestamp consistency, saved-baseline comparison, and machine-readable recommendations summarized from privacy-safe refresh history, store metadata, store read baseline, recent store table read metrics, cache stats, and API JSON cache stats without raw refresh debug fields

post.summary: Remove derived cache/report/store files without touching Minecraft logs

get.summary: Privacy-safe local diagnostics, optionally with full local paths
Privacy-safe troubleshooting package with app status, diagnostics, refresh history summaries without raw current files, errors, logs, or log tails, rule-pack validation, manifest, and privacy audit
Privacy-safe aggregate stats package for sharing with manifest and privacy audit
Owner aggregate and raw local account stats
Local ID playtime ranking
One local account or owner aggregate
Paginated source/client groups
Paginated version/scope groups
Paginated daily report rows
Rule coverage, cache stats, unmatched categories, and rule quality summary

post.summary: Classify one raw chat message using current rules
Generate a first-pass rule from one raw chat message
Generate a draft rule pack from reviewed label rows
Validate one inline custom rule pack

get.summary: Rule pack lifecycle and quality diagnostics

post.summary: Preview rule changes without writing report, store, or config data; includes a promotionGate for audited rule changes
Validate reviewed unknown-audit labels, generate a draft rule pack, and optionally dry-run it without enabling rules

get.summary: Bundled and configured custom rule pack metadata
Validate configured custom rule packs
List project-managed user rule packs

post.summary: Validate and save a project-managed user rule pack
Enable or disable a project-managed user rule pack in local config
List project-managed user rule pack backups
Restore a project-managed user rule pack from backup

get.summary: Read one project-managed user rule pack

delete.summary: Delete one project-managed user rule pack

get.summary: Split report store manifest, or store_not_ready/store_invalid_json/store_invalid_manifest when derived store data is absent or malformed
Paginated rows from a JSONL table declared in the split report store manifest
Time series rows
Unmatched debug data

post.summary: Run refresh preflight checks without starting a refresh job

get.summary: Recent privacy-safe rule lifecycle audit entries for save, enable, restore, draft, and dry-run operations

post.summary: Validate and summarize reviewed unknown-audit label rows without changing report, store, config, or rules
Return the next-step readiness status for reviewed unknown-audit label rows without drafting or dry-running rules

400.description: Invalid reviewed label rows or stale round refs

get.summary: List local derived unknown-audit label sets saved by the review UI without changing report, store, config, or rules

post.summary: Save a local derived unknown-audit label set for frontend/manual review drafts

200.description: Saved local unknown-audit label set with validation/readiness summary

get.summary: Read one local derived unknown-audit label set and its current validation/readiness summary

put.summary: Replace one local derived unknown-audit label set

delete.summary: Delete one local derived unknown-audit label set without touching report, store, config, rules, or raw logs

customRules.description: Safe project-relative custom rule pack directories or .json files.

dataDir.description: Safe project-relative derived-data directory; must not target Minecraft roots, config files, rule packs, source/docs directories, or package metadata.

outputs.description: Derived report output names. Values must be distinct project-relative .json paths and must not target config files, rule packs, source/docs directories, or package metadata.

report.description: Project-relative .json path for the full report output.

summary.description: Project-relative .json path for the summary report output.

encoding.description: Optional TextDecoder-supported encoding label, for example gb18030 or utf-8.

validate.description: When true, validate the selected path as a Minecraft log root before returning.

encoding.description: Optional TextDecoder-supported encoding label used only when validate is true.

path.description: Selected local directory path when ok is true.

cancelled.description: True when the user dismissed the native picker without choosing a folder.

validation.description: Root validation result included only when validate=true and a path was selected.

ruleSets.description: Optional known rule set ids to limit matching for this test request.

customRulePaths.description: Optional safe project-relative rule-pack directories or .json files used only for this test request.

resultSignal.description: True when this rule directly emitted a win/loss chat event in the current report.

resultEvidence.description: True when this rule appears in official win/loss result evidence for at least one reliable round.

boundarySignal.description: True when this rule produced round/session boundary or mode/population signals.

ruleSet.description: Effective legacy rule set key used by result evidence and backward-compatible rule selection.

rulePack.description: Physical rule pack id that contains this rule after mode-based bundled rule splitting.

key.description: Stable physical rule key in rulePack:ruleId form.

serverNetwork.description: Human-readable server network such as Hypixel or NetEase when known.

serverAddress.description: Server host or host:nonDefaultPort from a direct connection log when available.

serverLabel.description: Primary display label for round lists and details.

bedDestroys.description: All observed bed-destroy events in the round or aggregate.

selfBedDestroys.description: Compatibility field for beds destroyed by the owner/player.

playerBedDestroys.description: Recommended public/API field for beds destroyed by the owner/player; equals selfBedDestroys.

playerMaxKillStreak.description: Highest confirmed owner/player kill streak within this round or activity segment; self deaths and segment/round boundaries reset it. Broadcast streak messages for other players do not affect this field.

win.description: Win streaks over formal result-eligible rounds. CamelCase keys are primary for the frontend; snake_case aliases mirror the policy ids.

playerMaxKillStreak.description: Highest confirmed owner/player kill streak in the activity segment.

observedBroadcastMaxKillStreak.description: Highest kill-streak number observed in server chat broadcasts; this may belong to another player and is not a personal metric.

rewardEvents.description: Count of explicit activity reward prompt events, such as The Pit kill/assist rewards, gold pickups, free XP, death-streak rewards, or streak-point awards. This is an event count, not a gold/XP amount.

goldEarned.description: Sum of explicit The Pit gold amounts parsed from player reward chat lines and owner-bound bounty claims. This is not a full economy ledger.

xpEarned.description: Sum of explicit The Pit XP amounts parsed from player reward chat lines. This is not a full economy ledger.

bountyClaims.description: Count of The Pit bounty-claim broadcasts where the killer is confirmed to be the owner/player.

bountyGoldEarned.description: Gold earned from owner-bound The Pit bounty-claim broadcasts. This value is included in goldEarned.

scope.description: Metric ownership or aggregation scope such as player, observed_server_chat, activity, result_eligible_rounds, or formal_result_rounds.

MetricDefinitions.description: Machine-readable explanations for user-facing metrics so clients do not confuse player metrics with observed broadcasts or non-result activity rows.

validateRoundRefs.description: When true, rows with roundRef must still exist in the current reliable unknown report queue.

targetMode.description: Optional target game mode used by the dry-run promotion gate.

skipDryRun.description: When true, stop after label validation and draft generation.

full.description: When true, dry-run samples may include local-only line references.

validateRoundRefs.description: When true, rows with roundRef must still exist in the current reliable unknown report queue.

readyForWorkflow.description: True when the reviewed batch is complete enough to either run the workflow or archive as keep-unknown evidence.

workflowRecommended.description: True when draftable rule rows exist and the next step should be the combined audit workflow/dry-run preview.

missingRuleTextRows.description: Rows labeled win, loss, or ignore that cannot draft rules yet because no exact message/text field was supplied.

artifacts.description: Local artifact paths when artifact writing is enabled. API calls currently keep this empty.

labelSet.description: False for validation, status, draft, dry-run, and workflow APIs; saving label-set drafts uses UnknownAuditLabelSetWrites instead.

artifacts.description: True only for CLI/local workflow calls that explicitly write workflow artifacts.

dryRunCache.description: Dry-run may write isolated preview caches under derived data; it never writes official report/store/config/rules.

missingRuleTextRows.description: Number of win/loss/ignore labels that need an exact message field before rule drafting.

nextStep.description: Machine-readable next action for the reviewed label batch.

blocked.description: True when the workflow cannot safely proceed without fixing labels, exporting a queue, or adding human review input.

nextCommand.description: Suggested local CLI command template for the next backend step.

AuditWorkflowArtifactSummary.description: Privacy-safe local artifact summary. Paths are project-relative or file names, never absolute local paths.

targetMode.description: Optional target mode used by promotionGate to separate target unknown deltas from non-target mode changes.

auditExport.description: Optional source export filename or logical name.

labelSet.description: True only for the derived local label-set file.

filePath.description: Local derived file path; keep local-only.
